subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral + independent (IMF, World Bank, US GAO/CRS, Freedom House, Lowy) + international press; state media EXCLUDED. Tiny (~105k population) and data-thin; press not separately RSF-ranked. Fully dollarized (uses the US dollar).

An exceptionally strong sovereign balance sheet (trust-fund assets ~510% of GDP, debt ~9%) paired with weak federal cohesion (stalled Chuuk secessionism, March 2025 Chuuk political violence) and a renewed ~$3.3bn COFA.
Standout features: a Free democracy and an enormous fiscal buffer, against fragile four-state cohesion, very low capacity, chronic outmigration, and acute dispersed-atoll climate exposure.

economic.notes: A remarkably strong balance sheet against stagnant growth and negligible export diversity; fully dollarized.

fiscal_state.notes: Trust-fund assets ~510% of GDP and debt ~8.9% of GDP - very strong buffers despite a narrowing surplus. (IMF 2026 Art IV)

inflation.notes: Slowed to ~3.2% (FY25) as commodity prices and US inflation eased. (IMF 2026)

banking_stability.notes: 'Sound' per the IMF but with rising NPLs flagged; correspondent-banking de-risking and thin credit access are structural. (IMF 2026)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Reliant on fishing-license fees plus COFA grants, with negligible export diversity and credit constrained by informality. (IMF 2026)

institutional.notes: A Free rating and independent judiciary undercut by Chuuk political violence and very low capacity.

rule_of_law.notes: An independent judiciary and Free rating, but Chuuk political violence/arrests (Mar 2025) and weak enforcement reduce it. (FH 2025; IMF)

statistical_integrity.notes: Persistent data gaps; the IMF urges closing 'information gaps'. (IMF 2026)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Very low - a four-state federal structure with delayed infrastructure rollout and audit/disbursement problems. (GAO; IMF 2026)

press_freedom.notes: Freedom House: civil liberties generally respected; not RSF-ranked, with small/limited media. (FH 2025)

political_social.notes: Stable nationally but with recurring secessionist strain and Chuuk-level factional violence.

political_stability.notes: Stable democracy nationally, but recurring secessionist strain and Chuuk-level violence/factionalism. (FH 2025; Pacific Island Times 2025)

social_cohesion.notes: Weak inter-state cohesion (Yap/Chuuk/Pohnpei/Kosrae), recurrent Chuuk secession talk, and deep factionalism. (Nationalia; FH)

demographics.notes: Heavy, sustained emigration to the US (population ~105k and falling), with labor shortages. (IMF; GAO)

geopolitical.notes: US-aligned and COFA-secured, less of a militarized frontline than Palau.

alliance_reliability.notes: Stayed US-aligned after flirting with China; the renewed COFA secures it, with the near-term dinged by US-funding delays. (GAO; RNZ; State Dept)

conflict_involvement.notes: Remote and not a militarized frontline like Palau - low direct conflict exposure. (inference)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: Open, dollarized, and unsanctioned; de-risking the main friction. (IMF)

physical_practical.notes: Acute dispersed-atoll climate exposure and weak, remote services.

climate.notes: Mixed high islands plus atolls - SLR/coastal-erosion and typhoon exposure, low adaptive capacity. (WB Pacific CCDR; IPCC AR6)

healthcare.notes: Weak and dispersed across four states, with serious cases referred off-island. (inference)

infrastructure.notes: Remote, dispersed geography with delayed new projects and thin connectivity. (IMF 2026; GAO)

crime_safety.notes: Generally low violent crime, but Chuuk political violence is a localized exception. (FH; Pacific Island Times)

disaster_insurance.notes: A thin market against high exposure, donor-dependent. (inference)

summary: FSM pairs a remarkably strong sovereign balance sheet - trust-fund assets near 510% of GDP, debt under 9% - with structurally weak governance: a loose four-state federation with persistent Chuuk secessionism (now stalled but recently violent), very low civil-service capacity, and chronic outmigration. The renewed ~$3.3bn COFA secures long-term financing and the country has stayed firmly US-aligned, but FY24 funding delays forced bridge drawdowns and growth has slowed to ~1.1% amid implementation bottlenecks. Climate exposure across dispersed low islands is acute. The skew is symmetric: an exceptional fiscal cushion is genuinely offset by fragile cohesion, thin capacity, and demographic erosion.
